commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The next action is a contributor proof update or maintainer proof-acceptance decision; no narrow code repair is identified from this review.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff narrows existing workflow execution and adds no new actions, permissions, dependencies, secret handling, or untrusted checkout path. (sha=3cd59b1428ba2eb226ddb8e8673850588ce705c2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds field-specific `pull_request_target` edited guards to PR automation workflows and routes those workflow edits to guard coverage tests.

PR surface: Tests +58, Config +38, Other +4. Total +100 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an automation optimization rather than a reported runtime bug. Source review and workflow/test inspection give a high-confidence review path, but the proposed edited-event matrix was not fully exercised in a live or accepted synthetic setup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is a contributor proof update or maintainer proof-acceptance decision; no narrow code repair is identified from this review.

target.title: ci: gate PR edit automation by changed field
target.body:
## Summary
- gate PR `edited` automation on the fields each workflow actually consumes
- keep ClawSweeper dispatch active for title/body/base edits because it serializes PR title into its activity payload
- skip expensive PR automation for metadata-only edits while preserving title/body/base behavior
- add workflow guard coverage and changed-test routing for the edited-field matrix

## What gets optimized
The optimization is: when a PR is edited, do not wake every PR automation job just because any PR metadata changed. Instead, each job wakes only when the edited field can change that job's output.

- `Auto response` (`auto-response`): wakes for title, body, or base edits. It reads PR text and branch/label context to decide whether an automatic response should change.
- `ClawSweeper Dispatch` (`dispatch`): wakes for title, body, or base edits. It sends PR activity/review state to ClawSweeper, including the PR title.
- `Labeler` (`label`): wakes for title or base edits. Base changes can change path/size labels; title changes can change title-derived labels. Body-only edits do not affect labels.
- `Real behavior proof` (`Real behavior proof`): wakes for body or base edits. The proof checker reads the PR body and target context; title-only edits do not affect proof content.

Metadata-only edits, such as milestone/assignee/project-style changes that none of these jobs read, should skip these expensive PR edited-event jobs.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs .github/workflows/auto-response.yml .github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml .github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: PR `pull_request_target` `edited` automation now wakes only for fields each workflow reads. ClawSweeper dispatch includes `title`, `body`, and `base`; auto-response includes `title`, `body`, and `base`; labeler includes `title` and `base`; real-behavior-proof includes `body` and `base`.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `optimize-pr-edit-automation` at commit `3cd59b1428`; draft PR #94682 body update on GitHub.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts
node scripts/check-workflows.mjs .github/workflows/auto-response.yml .github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml .github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  189 passed (189)
zizmor...................................................................Passed
No direct inputs interpolation found in composite run blocks.
```

Observed result after fix: parsed workflow guard coverage now proves the edited-field matrix. Title-only edits dispatch ClawSweeper; body/base edits dispatch ClawSweeper; metadata-only edits do not satisfy the guarded expensive PR jobs. No persistent data-model or vector/embedding metadata files changed; the only test change is workflow guard coverage.

What was not tested: a full live GitHub `pull_request_target` edited-event matrix for the proposed workflow file before merge. GitHub evaluates `pull_request_target` workflow definitions from the target branch, so the proposed guard change cannot be authoritatively exercised on this PR until it is on a target branch.
target.author: zats
